reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We have to produce plan first. Use review_plan first. We need to address rule: ensure net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra=0 in persistent file, with high priority and proper ownership and permissions. Also comment existing lines. We will pick /etc/sysctl.d/90-sshd-disable-ra.conf or 90-disable-ra.conf. Use name 90-disable-ra.conf. Steps:

1. Read any file containing net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra uncommented; use grep. If it exists, comment it. Since we don't run grep as tool? We can run run_cmd for grep via mod? We can just check via read_file? But reading many files is heavy. Maybe use run_cmd to grep and redirect? But we need to use tools for commands. We can read individual files. Let's keep simpler: We'll overwrite the setting by creating our own config file. But need to ensure existing uncommented lines set to 0? We can comment them by editing all /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf, /run/sysctl.d/*.conf, /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf.

Plan:

- For each file in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf, /run/sysctl.d/*.conf, /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf (excluding symlinks to /etc/sysctl.conf), run sed to comment out any uncommented line matching pattern ^\s*net\.ipv6\.conf\.default\.accept_ra\b

- After that, create /etc/sysctl.d/90-disable-ra.conf with content: net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra = 0

- Set ownership root:root and permissions 0644

- Apply the setting runtime: run sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/90-disable-ra.conf or sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra=0

We may need to run multiple run_cmds sequentially, each separately. But they want us to use tools and not chain commands.

Given we have limited number of commands.

We must ensure no duplicate line. Our new file will include exactly one line.

We also need to ensure that we don't comment out any lines that are already set to 0? It doesn't matter.

Ok.

We first open review_plan with plan description.

Let's do that.
